theme: infer a pressure vessel's leak law from patchy logs
title: Pressure Vessel Leak Forensics — recovering a leak exponent behind unlogged deliveries
strategies: trivial: force alpha=1 (proportional leak), fit k from raw uncorrected daily deltas -> reproduces the checker's own baseline, ~0.1

greedy: treat every day's net level change as pure decay (ignore the daily delivery total entirely), fit a power law by OLS to log(level) vs log(apparent rate) -- looks reasonable, even nails a few low-delivery instances, but on delivery-heavy instances the unmodelled deliveries masquerade as suppressed decay and drag the fitted exponent/constant far from truth, blowing up under extrapolation

strong: the insight -- delivery TIMES are unobserved and unrecoverable, but mass balance over a full day is exact regardless of timing: decay_lost = Q_d + E_(d-1) - E_d, on EVERY day, charge or not. This deconvolves the hidden delivery events from the decay signal via bookkeeping alone (no curve flexibility, no event-time estimation), then a weighted log-log regression on the recovered decay-only signal recovers the exponent cleanly and generalises to the held-out high-level regime

headroom above strong: recover the within-day delivery timing itself (not just its net effect) via a joint nonlinear fit against the exact closed-form decay solution, squeezing out the residual bias the linear mass-balance proxy leaves in the representative level per day
